prompt: In a IPv4 & Subnetting environment, investigate name resolution failure. Explain scope, dependencies, ranked hypotheses, tests, evidence, corrective action, validation, and prevention.

answer_framework.scope: Identify affected users, hosts, VLANs, sites, protocols, and the exact failure window.
answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed name resolution failure is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with traceroute and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed asymmetric routing is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with tcpdump and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed high latency is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show ip route and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed duplicate IP behavior is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show spanning-tree and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed route missing from table is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with traceroute and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed adjacency stuck is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with tcpdump and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed TLS validation failure is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show ip route and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed application timeout is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with show spanning-tree and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed unexpected firewall deny is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with traceroute and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.

answer_framework.dependencies: Map L1/L2/L3, name resolution, policy, stateful inspection, translation, certificates, and application dependencies as applicable.
hypotheses: Configuration or state related to IPv4 & Subnetting is inconsistent with the intended production path.
The observed NAT translation missing is secondary to an upstream dependency failure.
Forward and return paths differ, or stateful devices see only one direction.
A recent change introduced policy, routing, addressing, timing, or certificate mismatch.
tests: Capture baseline evidence with tcpdump and preserve timestamps.
Compare a failing path with a known-good path.
Test hop-by-hop and layer-by-layer instead of changing multiple variables.
Validate counters, logs, packet flow, route selection, and return path.
answer_framework.validation: Repeat the original user transaction, verify telemetry and counters, monitor recurrence, and document RCA.
answer_framework.interview_tip: State assumptions, avoid random command dumping, explain why each test separates competing hypotheses.
